title: modalDistribution — streaming reducer over JSON-lines
description: Implement a function `modalDistribution(input: string) => { Asserted, Hypothetical, Counterfactual, other, total }` that reads newline-separated JSON, counts the `modalStatus` field per descriptor, skips malformed JSON lines without crashing, and treats unknown / missing modal status as `other`. Empty input returns all zeros. Output ALL counters, even if zero.

tests.summary: 5 cases: empty input, single Asserted, mixed three categories, malformed-line-skip, unknown-modal-as-other

success_criteria: node --test exits 0 against all five test cases
implementation_file exports a single named function `modalDistribution`
no crash on malformed JSON; malformed lines are silently skipped
hints_for_agent: The function takes a STRING (raw JSON-lines), not an array or stream
Splitting on '\n' is fine; each line is one JSON object
Use try/catch around JSON.parse for malformed-line resilience
All four modal counters MUST appear in the return value even when zero

reuse_signal.rationale: There may already be similar manifest-counting helpers in the repo. Agent-beta is rewarded for searching FIRST and reusing patterns rather than writing from scratch — the calibration-driven replan exercises this divergence between alpha and beta.
